mechanism_narrative: A persistent decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, mechanically lifting equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises above 5.8 percent within a single month, labor market deterioration triggers immediate risk-off positioning by institutional investors, depressing S&P 500 prices over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises above 5.8 percent in a single month
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in more than 65 percent of instances where UNRATE crosses 5.8 percent, or the signal shows no predictive power in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous acceleration in industrial production and growth in real retail sales indicates synchronized supply-demand recovery, which expands corporate profit margins and drives positive S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal yields a negative or statistically insignificant correlation with 60-day forward SPX returns across three consecutive rolling 5-year periods.

mechanism_narrative: During periods where the federal funds rate exceeds 4.5 percent, elevated borrowing costs compress equity multiples and force deleveraging, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward 20-day SPX returns are positive in more than 60 percent of observations when FEDFUNDS remains above 4.5 percent for two consecutive quarters.

mechanism_narrative: Rising real retail sales volume directly reflects stronger consumer spending, which improves near-term corporate revenue guidance and lifts S&P 500 valuations over the subsequent 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC for RSAFS changes against 5-day forward SPX returns falls below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, indicating no persistent predictive relationship.

mechanism_narrative: When the industrial production index breaks above 112.0, manufacturing capacity utilization signals robust economic momentum, which sustains positive S&P 500 returns over the following 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns are negative or flat in more than 55 percent of instances following an INDPRO breakout above 112.0.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrently rising unemployment and falling housing starts indicate a synchronized downturn in both labor and housing markets, which amplifies recession fears and drives negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined signal fails to predict negative 20-day SPX returns in more than 50 percent of triggered episodes over a 10-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: In a low-inflation environment where CPI remains below 2.5 percent, rising real retail sales boost equity valuations without triggering rate hike expectations, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL annualized inflation rate falls below 2.5 percent
failure_condition: Forward 5-day SPX returns are negative in more than 60 percent of cases when CPI stays below 2.5 percent and retail sales rise simultaneously.
